steps.title: Find the key causal contrast
steps.content:
The passage's key signal is the contrast between two competing explanations: Philip's defaults were caused by short-term liquidity crises, not long-term unsustainable debts.

This reveals the research's value—newly compiled evidence that eliminates one explanation and confirms another.

steps.title: Match to the answer
steps.content: Choice A — Marquez and Richter's research uses newly compiled evidence to eliminate one possible reason Philip II defaulted on his debts and to affirm another. captures this exactly: they used newly compiled evidence to eliminate one possible reason Philip defaulted and affirm another. The remaining choices either reverse the causal relationship or misidentify the source of the hypothesis. That matches Choice A — Marquez and Richter's research uses newly compiled evidence to eliminate one possible reason Philip II defaulted on his debts and to affirm another..
